title: Playground Library Desk
concept: A saved-sites-first enterprise dashboard where the first viewport is a dense browser-backed Playground library, creation and transfer workflows are actions on that library, and the active WordPress site remains secondary inside a contextual drawer or selected-site manager.
focus: saved-sites library as the primary workspace
thin-border tables instead of modal-first panels
power user management of many Playgrounds
flows: Start from a library of saved and unsaved Playgrounds, filter rows, select a site, then open, rename, delete, save, or manage it from the contextual drawer.
Create a new Playground from Vanilla WordPress, WordPress PR, Gutenberg PR or branch, GitHub import, Blueprint URL, local zip import, featured Blueprints, or the full Blueprint gallery.
Save a temporary Playground to browser storage or a local directory with visible file-copy progress and stored reload behavior.
Manage the selected site through Settings, File browser, Blueprint editor, Database, Logs, Homepage, WP Admin, path navigation, and refresh controls.
Move work through GitHub import, GitHub export, zip import, zip download, Blueprint copy/download/run, and database.sqlite download.
featureCoverage: create from Gutenberg PR or branch
import from GitHub after account connection
browse and filter Blueprint gallery entries
save temporary Playground in browser storage
navigate active WordPress paths with URL input
refresh the active embedded WordPress page
understand destructive reset and stored Save & Reload behavior
use File browser with create folder/file, upload, browse, and code editing affordances
